description: Sadaharu Aoki brings his Paris training to Tokyo with a refined galette featuring delicate almond cream and perfectly laminated puff pastry. Known for blending French tradition with Japanese precision.
highlight: Matcha-infused galette variant available some years, a unique Aoki signature

description: Pierre Herme's galette is a masterclass in frangipane with an intensely buttery, caramelized pastry shell. The flavor combinations change annually with creative twists.
highlight: Collectible feves designed exclusively for Pierre Herme, highly sought by collectors

address: Ebisu Garden Place, Mita, Meguro-ku, Tokyo
description: Robuchon's boutique offers a classically styled galette with rich almond frangipane and golden, flaky pastry layers. Elegant presentation befitting the brand.
highlight: Luxurious packaging and refined feve design reflecting Robuchon's attention to detail

description: The creator of the Cronut brings an inventive take on galette des rois with generous almond cream and an exceptionally caramelized crust. Limited quantities sell out fast.
highlight: Creative annual feve designs and the signature Dominique Ansel flaky pastry technique

description: A beloved Tomigaya patisserie run by the Tsuchiya sisters, offering a straightforward and well-executed galette with rich almond filling. Approachable pricing for the quality.
highlight: Homey, authentic French style at accessible prices in a charming neighborhood setting

description: A butter-focused patisserie that puts premium French butter at the center of its galette. The puff pastry is exceptionally rich and layered.
highlight: Butter connoisseurs consider this one of the best in Tokyo for pure pastry quality

description: Chef Teranishi trained in France and brings authentic technique to his galette. Known for perfectly balanced frangipane with a hint of rum.
highlight: Authentic Parisian-style galette from a chef with deep French patisserie training

description: VIRON uses French flour imported from Viron mill for its galette, giving the puff pastry a distinctive wheaty depth. A boulangerie-style approach with substance.
highlight: Uses genuine French Viron flour, the same used in top Paris bakeries

description: Chef Kaneko's galette features beautifully laminated pastry with a classic French frangipane. The shop itself evokes a quiet Parisian morning.
highlight: Elegant hand-finished crown design and balanced, not-too-sweet almond cream

description: Located on the French-flavored streets of Kagurazaka, RYOCO offers a galette that balances traditional French technique with subtle Japanese sensibility.
highlight: Kagurazaka's French quarter atmosphere adds to the galette experience

description: Chef Takagi is known for meticulous French technique and sources high-quality almonds for his frangipane. Consistently ranks among Tokyo's best galettes.
highlight: Beautifully caramelized top with intricate traditional scoring patterns

description: Maison Kayser brings accessible French boulangerie-style galette to multiple Tokyo locations. Reliable quality and easy availability make it a popular entry point.
highlight: Widely available across Tokyo with consistent quality and approachable pricing

description: The Park Hyatt's pastry boutique creates a luxurious galette with premium ingredients and impeccable presentation. A splurge-worthy celebration piece.
highlight: Premium hotel-quality presentation with exclusive feve designs each year

description: The Mandarin Oriental's pastry team crafts an elegant galette with rich frangipane and buttery layers. Often features a unique twist ingredient each year.
highlight: Annual limited-edition feves and sophisticated flavor variations

description: Palace Hotel Tokyo's galette reflects the hotel's refined aesthetic with a classic French approach and exceptional butter pastry. Popular for gifting.
highlight: Beautifully boxed and suitable for formal gifting occasions

description: SATSUKI's galette is made by pastry chefs known for their super-premium desserts. Rich almond cream with a deep caramel finish on the pastry.
highlight: From the team behind SATSUKI's famous super melon shortcake

shop_name: Grand Hyatt Tokyo - Fiorentina Pastry Boutique
description: The Fiorentina Pastry Boutique offers a polished galette with generous frangipane and golden, shattering puff pastry. Roppongi Hills location is convenient for pickup.
highlight: Stylish Roppongi Hills location and consistently well-executed pastry

shop_name: The Peninsula Tokyo - The Peninsula Boutique & Cafe
description: The Peninsula's galette is a refined showpiece with meticulous lamination and fragrant almond cream. Limited quantities make early reservation essential.
highlight: Exquisite packaging and limited production numbers add to exclusivity

description: Chef Ujita's galette showcases his classical French training with precise lamination and fragrant Sicilian almond frangipane. A local favorite in Ogikubo.
highlight: Artisan attention to detail with hand-selected almonds and imported French butter

description: A neighborhood patisserie with a loyal following for its honest, well-made galette. Good balance of buttery pastry and almond filling without being overly sweet.
highlight: Neighborhood gem with loyal regulars who pre-order every January

description: A legendary Tokyo patisserie by Chef Kawaguchi, known for decades of classical French pastry. The galette follows strict traditional methods with no shortcuts.
highlight: One of Tokyo's most revered patisseries with over 40 years of French tradition

description: Chef Hatae's galette features a distinctive deep-golden finish with aromatic frangipane. Trained in France, his approach emphasizes purity of flavor.
highlight: Known for importing feves directly from French artisan makers

description: Made entirely with Echire AOP butter, this galette is a butter lover's dream. The pastry layers are extraordinarily rich and the frangipane is deeply fragrant.
highlight: 100% Echire AOP butter creates arguably the most buttery galette in Tokyo

description: A respected Kichijoji boulangerie that offers a rustic-style galette with hearty puff pastry and generous almond filling. Casual atmosphere with serious baking.
highlight: Casual boulangerie vibe with surprisingly refined galette technique

description: An east Tokyo patisserie gaining recognition for its carefully made galette with well-balanced sweetness and perfectly flaky layers.
highlight: Rising star in Tokyo's galette scene, worth the trip to the east side

description: Dalloyau's 300-year heritage shines through in their traditional Parisian galette. Classic frangipane with the confidence of a house that has been making this for centuries.
highlight: Historic Parisian maison with centuries of galette-making tradition

description: A well-known Japanese patisserie brand offering a reliably good galette with smooth frangipane. Multiple locations make it accessible across Tokyo.
highlight: Wide availability across Japan and consistent quality from a trusted brand

description: The Ritz-Carlton's galette is a showpiece with premium ingredients and meticulous pastry work. One of the most luxurious options in Tokyo.
highlight: Top-tier luxury presentation with some of the most elaborate feve designs

description: Conticini's inventive pastry approach brings a modern sensibility to the galette, with layered textures and nuanced almond cream.
highlight: Innovative pastry techniques from one of France's most creative patissiers

description: Chef Tsujiguchi's galette benefits from his mastery of sugar work and pastry. Beautifully decorated with a frangipane that has depth and complexity.
highlight: From one of Japan's most decorated pastry chefs, known for competition-level technique

description: A French-style boulangerie in Osaka bringing authentic galette des rois to the Kansai region. Uses high-quality French flour and butter.
highlight: One of Osaka's most authentic French boulangeries for traditional galette

description: A charming Osaka patisserie in the trendy Nakazakicho area that takes its galette seriously, with traditional scoring and imported French feves.
highlight: Located in one of Osaka's most walkable, cafe-filled neighborhoods

description: A Kyoto patisserie offering galette des rois with a refined sensibility that blends French technique with Kyoto's aesthetic traditions.
highlight: Kyoto's understated elegance applied to a French classic

description: A Kyoto patisserie with French roots, serving a classic galette with well-balanced frangipane and golden puff pastry in the Kitayama area.
highlight: Kitayama's tree-lined boulevard setting adds to the French patisserie atmosphere
